extra.message: User-controlled query parameter 'username' is concatenated directly into a Jinja2 template string via Environment().from_string().render(). This is Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI). An attacker can inject arbitrary Jinja2 template expressions/payloads (e.g., ?username={{ 7*7 }}, ?username={{ ''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__() }}) to achieve remote code execution. The Environment() on line 11 is created with no sandbox restrictions and autoescaping is disabled by default.

The rendered template output is returned via HTMLResponse without Content-Type sanitization or output encoding. User-controlled content from the 'username' parameter is served as text/html, enabling Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via inline JavaScript (e.g., ?username=<script>alert('xss')</script>). Additionally, autoescaping is disabled because Jinja2 Environment() on line 11 defaults to autoescape=None.
